name: Easy Beef Roll-Ups
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the round steak into strips that are 1 inch wide and 4 inches long.
2. Trim excess fat from the steak and bacon.
3. Place bacon slices on top of the steak strips and roll them up, securing with small wooden picks.
4. Heat oil in a large frying pan and brown the roll-ups slowly.
5. Transfer the roll-ups to a pressure cooker.
6. Add the soup mix and 2 cups of water to the frying pan and simmer for 3 minutes, scraping any particles from the bottom of the pan.
7. Pour the liquid over the roll-ups and cook under high pressure for 10 minutes.
8. Remove the roll-ups to a warm serving dish.
9. Mix cornstarch with half a cup of water until smooth.
10. Add this mixture to the liquid in the pressure cooker and simmer, stirring constantly, for 3 to 4 minutes or until thickened.
11. Pour the gravy over the roll-ups and garnish with parsley and cherry tomatoes.
